seed_problem: Given alphabet size a, factor length k, and a forbidden set F of length-k strings, output one string S that contains no forbidden factor; maximize the number of distinct ALLOWED length-k factors it covers.
mutation: composite: reformulate maximum k-mer coverage as a constrained MAX-EDGE walk on a MUTILATED de Bruijn graph. Forbidden factors delete edges individually, so the survivor is deliberately UN-balanced (many local sinks); a naive/greedy single trail stalls at a sink and strands most reachable factors, while the strong solution takes the largest strongly-connected component, Eulerizes it by repeating a few allowed edges, and sweeps all its edges with one Eulerian circuit.
theme: Cover k-mers while dodging forbidden factors
title: Forbidden Factory: Maximal k-mer Coverage on a Mutilated de Bruijn Graph
innovation_hook: Forbidden factors turn a clean Eulerian cover into a max-edge rural-postman walk on a mutilated de Bruijn graph; the strong solution reformulates coverage as an Eulerian trail and splices leftover cycles with Hierholzer.
strategies: naive lexicographic greedy walk (baseline): start at the smallest legal vertex, always append the smallest allowed-and-unused symbol, stop when stuck at a sink -- strands most factors

best-of-many-starts trail with dead-end avoidance: try many start vertices, at each step prefer the edge whose target has the most remaining unused out-edges; reaches further but, as a single trail, still stalls at a sink and cannot recover edges behind it

max-edge-walk reformulation (strong): take the largest strongly-connected component, Eulerize it with a few repeated allowed edges (repeats are free since coverage counts DISTINCT factors), and sweep every edge with one Eulerian circuit; keep whichever of this circuit or a long trail covers more
trap: dumping a full de Bruijn sequence ignores the forbidden set, contains a forbidden factor, and is disqualified
